Here are my findings:\n\n---\n\n## Code Quality Review — app/src/\n\n### HIGH SEVERITY\n\n**1. `app/src/lib/palette.ts:9` — Division by zero when sMod === 0**\n`getSaturation` returns `1` when `sMod === 0`, but `pctToSMod(0)` in `AdjustmentSlider.tsx` also returns `0`. The slider range is `-100` to `0`, and when the user drags to `0` (max), `pctToSMod(0)` returns `0`, which is passed as `sMod`. Then `getSaturation(l, 0)` would divide by zero — `Math.pow(50, 2) / 0`. The early return `if (sMod === 0) return 1` guards this correctly, so it's not a crash, but the semantics are inverted: `sMod=0` means \"no curve\" and returns the baseline multiplier of 1. This is actually correct behavior, but the guard condition is essential and not documented. Low risk, but a latent hazard if the guard is ever removed.\n\n**2. `app/src/components/About.tsx:71` — Forward reference to `LIGHTNESS_LABELS` before declaration**\n`BOUNDARY_SCALE` is declared at line 71 and references `LIGHTNESS_LABELS` (line 66) and `getSaturation`. JavaScript hoisting means `const` declarations are NOT hoisted — but `LIGHTNESS_LABELS` at line 66 is declared before line 71, so this is fine at runtime. However `BOUNDARY_SCALE` is used inside `buildStrips` (line 53), which is declared at line 39 — *before* `BOUNDARY_SCALE`. Since `buildStrips` is only called at render time (not at module init), this works, but it's fragile and confusing. The ordering of module-level constants should be top-to-bottom by dependency.\n\n**3. `app/src/hooks/useCopyToClipboard.ts:9` — Unhandled clipboard promise rejection**\n`navigator.clipboard.writeText(text).then(...)` has no `.catch()`. If the clipboard API fails (e.g., permissions denied, non-secure context, browser restriction), the error is silently swallowed. There's no user feedback for the failure case.\n\n---\n\n### MEDIUM SEVERITY\n\n**4. `app/src/components/About.tsx` — `useCurvePickerVisuals` returns JSX from a hook**\nThe custom hook `useCurvePickerVisuals` (line 315) returns JSX elements (`curveGraph`, `rotatedPicker`). This is an anti-pattern — hooks should return data, not rendering. This makes the hook untestable, prevents memoization of the returned JSX, and blurs the separation between logic and presentation. These should be React components receiving the computed data as props.\n\n**5. `app/src/components/curves/SaturationCurve.tsx` & `EffectiveSaturation.tsx` — Duplicate SVG chart infrastructure**\nBoth files share identical constants (`W`, `H`, `PAD`, `PLOT_W`, `PLOT_H` — lines 11–15 in both), identical `toSvgX`/`toSvgY` coordinate transforms (redefined inline in each), and identical SVG grid/axis rendering markup. This is a significant duplication (~80 lines each). A shared `<ChartContainer>` component and `useChartCoords` hook would eliminate the repetition.\n\n**6. `app/src/components/palette/PaletteGrid.tsx:1` — `useMemo` imported but the toggle switch pattern duplicated**\nThe custom toggle switch (button with role=\"switch\") is copy-pasted 4 times in `PaletteGrid.tsx` (lines 96–127) and again in `SaturationCurve.tsx` and `EffectiveSaturation.tsx`. This is 6+ instances of identical markup with no shared component.\n\n**7. `app/src/components/export/ExportDialog.tsx:15` — `formatPalette` called unconditionally when dialog is closed**\n`const code = formatPalette(entries, format, space)` runs on every render of `ExportDialog`, even when `open === false`. The dialog is always mounted in the component tree (it's in `Header` which is always rendered). This means palette formatting runs on every store update even when the export dialog is not visible. It should be memoized with `useMemo` depending on `[entries, format, space]`, or deferred until the dialog is open.\n\n**8. `app/src/components/About.tsx` — `ColorPickerPlane` regenerates 1600 SVG cells on every hue change**\n`ColorPickerPlane` builds a 40×40 grid of `<rect>` elements (1600 cells) plus `boundaryCells` (40 cells with Framer Motion animations) on every render triggered by hue change. There's no `useMemo` anywhere in this component. With the hue picker being a drag interaction (continuous pointer events), this creates heavy re-renders per frame.\n\n**9. `app/src/components/About.tsx` — `useCurvePickerVisuals` also creates 1600 `pickerCells` without memoization**\nSame issue — 1600 cells for the rotated picker grid are computed on every render.\n\n---\n\n### LOW SEVERITY\n\n**10. `app/src/components/palette/PaletteGrid.tsx:10` — Magic number in STEP_OPTIONS**\n`STEP_OPTIONS = [8, 10, 12, 16, 20, 24, 48, 98]` — the value `98` is used as the default `swatchCount` (line 50). The default `generateNeutrals` produces 98 entries (from `min=0.03` to `max=1.01` at `distance=0.01`, filtered by `Math.floor(value * 100)` uniqueness). This coupling is implicit — if palette generation changes, the default count silently mismatches.\n\n**11. `app/src/components/palette/PaletteStrip.tsx:10` — Unnecessary spread + reverse**\n`[...entries].reverse()` creates a new array on every render. Since `entries` comes from `usePalette()` which is already memoized, this could use `useMemo` or the data could be pre-ordered. Minor, but worth noting.\n\n**12. `app/src/lib/palette.ts:34-35` — Overly complex rounding formula**\n`Math.round(Math.round((s + Number.EPSILON) * 100 * 100) / 100)` is equivalent to `Math.round(s * 100)` for any value that chroma.js returns. The double-round with epsilon guard is defensive but obscures intent and the outer `Math.round` is redundant since the inner already rounds to integer percents.\n\n**13. `app/src/components/About.tsx` — Inline `<div />` used as empty grid spacer (lines 700, 760)**\nTwo bare `<div />` elements are used as empty column fillers in a CSS grid layout. Per project CLAUDE.md guidelines: \"Never use empty divs for layout spacing — use proper margin/padding instead.\" These could be replaced with `grid-column` positioning on the adjacent element.\n\n**14. `app/src/components/controls/SliderBase.tsx:10` — Missing `React` import for `React.CSSProperties`**\n`trackStyle?: React.CSSProperties` references `React` in the type annotation, but `React` is not imported. This works because Vite's JSX transform doesn't require the import for JSX, but the type reference to `React.CSSProperties` requires `React` to be in scope for TypeScript. This likely works currently only because TypeScript's module resolution finds it globally, but it should be an explicit `import type React from 'react'` or use `CSSProperties` from `react` directly.\n\n**15. `app/src/components/About.tsx:87,88` — `Strip` component uses index as key**\n`{colors.map((c, i) => <div key={i} .../>)}` and `{colors.map((_, i) => ... key={i})}` — using array index as key is fine here since the list is fixed-length and never reordered, but worth flagging for consistency with the rest of the codebase which uses stable identifiers.\n\n---\n\n### SUMMARY TABLE\n\n| # | File | Line | Severity | Issue |\n|---|------|------|----------|-------|\n| 1 | lib/palette.ts | 9 | High | Division-by-zero guard is load-bearing but undocumented |\n| 2 | About.tsx | 39–71 | High | Module-level constant ordering creates fragile forward dependency |\n| 3 | hooks/useCopyToClipboard.ts | 9 | High | Unhandled clipboard API rejection |\n| 4 | About.tsx | 315 | Medium | Hook returns JSX — anti-pattern |\n| 5 | SaturationCurve.tsx + EffectiveSaturation.tsx | 11–15 | Medium | Duplicated chart constants and infrastructure |\n| 6 | PaletteGrid.tsx + SaturationCurve.tsx + EffectiveSaturation.tsx | various | Medium | Toggle switch markup copy-pasted 6+ times |\n| 7 | export/ExportDialog.tsx | 15 | Medium | formatPalette runs on every render even when dialog closed |\n| 8 | About.tsx | 128–295 | Medium | ColorPickerPlane: 1600 SVG rects + Framer Motion cells with no memoization |\n| 9 | About.tsx | 315–454 | Medium | useCurvePickerVisuals: 1600 cells without memoization |\n| 10 | palette/PaletteGrid.tsx | 10,50 | Low | Magic number coupling between default count and palette generation |\n| 11 | palette/PaletteStrip.tsx | 10 | Low | Unnecessary array spread+reverse on every render |\n| 12 | lib/palette.ts | 34–35 | Low | Redundant double-round formula |\n| 13 | About.tsx | 700,760 | Low | Empty divs used as CSS grid spacers (violates CLAUDE.md) |\n| 14 | controls/SliderBase.tsx | 10 | Low | React.CSSProperties used without explicit React import |\n| 15 | About.tsx | 87,88 | Low | Index used as key in Strip component maps |\n"
```text
  }
}
```
</details></tool-use>

_**User (2026-03-16 11:24:52Z)**_

<teammate-message teammate_id="code-reviewer" color="blue" summary="Code quality review complete — findings attached">
Code quality review complete. Here are my findings:

---

## Code Quality Review — app/src/

### HIGH SEVERITY

**1. `app/src/lib/palette.ts:9` — Division by zero when sMod === 0**
`getSaturation` returns `1` when `sMod === 0`, but `pctToSMod(0)` in `AdjustmentSlider.tsx` also returns `0`. The slider range is `-100` to `0`, and when the user drags to `0` (max), `pctToSMod(0)` returns `0`, which is passed as `sMod`. Then `getSaturation(l, 0)` would divide by zero — `Math.pow(50, 2) / 0`. The early return `if (sMod === 0) return 1` guards this correctly, so it's not a crash, but the semantics are inverted: `sMod=0` means "no curve" and returns the baseline multiplier of 1. This is actually correct behavior, but the guard condition is essential and not documented. Low risk, but a latent hazard if the guard is ever removed.

**2. `app/src/components/About.tsx:71` — Forward reference to `LIGHTNESS_LABELS` before declaration**
`BOUNDARY_SCALE` is declared at line 71 and references `LIGHTNESS_LABELS` (line 66) and `getSaturation`. JavaScript hoisting means `const` declarations are NOT hoisted — but `LIGHTNESS_LABELS` at line 66 is declared before line 71, so this is fine at runtime. However `BOUNDARY_SCALE` is used inside `buildStrips` (line 53), which is declared at line 39 — *before* `BOUNDARY_SCALE`. Since `buildStrips` is only called at render time (not at module init), this works, but it's fragile and confusing. The ordering of module-level constants should be top-to-bottom by dependency.

**3. `app/src/hooks/useCopyToClipboard.ts:9` — Unhandled clipboard promise rejection**
`navigator.clipboard.writeText(text).then(...)` has no `.catch()`. If the clipboard API fails (e.g., permissions denied, non-secure context, browser restriction), the error is silently swallowed. There's no user feedback for the failure case.

---

### MEDIUM SEVERITY

**4. `app/src/components/About.tsx` — `useCurvePickerVisuals` returns JSX from a hook**
The custom hook `useCurvePickerVisuals` (line 315) returns JSX elements (`curveGraph`, `rotatedPicker`). This is an anti-pattern — hooks should return data, not rendering. This makes the hook untestable, prevents memoization of the returned JSX, and blurs the separation between logic and presentation. These should be React components receiving the computed data as props.

**5. `app/src/components/curves/SaturationCurve.tsx` & `EffectiveSaturation.tsx` — Duplicate SVG chart infrastructure**
Both files share identical constants (`W`, `H`, `PAD`, `PLOT_W`, `PLOT_H` — lines 11–15 in both), identical `toSvgX`/`toSvgY` coordinate transforms (redefined inline in each), and identical SVG grid/axis rendering markup. This is a significant duplication (~80 lines each). A shared `<ChartContainer>` component and `useChartCoords` hook would eliminate the repetition.

**6. `app/src/components/palette/PaletteGrid.tsx:1` — `useMemo` imported but the toggle switch pattern duplicated**
The custom toggle switch (button with role="switch") is copy-pasted 4 times in `PaletteGrid.tsx` (lines 96–127) and again in `SaturationCurve.tsx` and `EffectiveSaturation.tsx`. This is 6+ instances of identical markup with no shared component.

**7. `app/src/components/export/ExportDialog.tsx:15` — `formatPalette` called unconditionally when dialog is closed**
`const code = formatPalette(entries, format, space)` runs on every render of `ExportDialog`, even when `open === false`. The dialog is always mounted in the component tree (it's in `Header` which is always rendered). This means palette formatting runs on every store update even when the export dialog is not visible. It should be memoized with `useMemo` depending on `[entries, format, space]`, or deferred until the dialog is open.

**8. `app/src/components/About.tsx` — `ColorPickerPlane` regenerates 1600 SVG cells on every hue change**
`ColorPickerPlane` builds a 40×40 grid of `<rect>` elements (1600 cells) plus `boundaryCells` (40 cells with Framer Motion animations) on every render triggered by hue change. There's no `useMemo` anywhere in this component. With the hue picker being a drag interaction (continuous pointer events), this creates heavy re-renders per frame.

**9. `app/src/components/About.tsx` — `useCurvePickerVisuals` also creates 1600 `pickerCells` without memoization**
Same issue — 1600 cells for the rotated picker grid are computed on every render.

---

### LOW SEVERITY

**10. `app/src/components/palette/PaletteGrid.tsx:10` — Magic number in STEP_OPTIONS**
`STEP_OPTIONS = [8, 10, 12, 16, 20, 24, 48, 98]` — the value `98` is used as the default `swatchCount` (line 50). The default `generateNeutrals` produces 98 entries (from `min=0.03` to `max=1.01` at `distance=0.01`, filtered by `Math.floor(value * 100)` uniqueness). This coupling is implicit — if palette generation changes, the default count silently mismatches.

**11. `app/src/components/palette/PaletteStrip.tsx:10` — Unnecessary spread + reverse**
`[...entries].reverse()` creates a new array on every render. Since `entries` comes from `usePalette()` which is already memoized, this could use `useMemo` or the data could be pre-ordered. Minor, but worth noting.

**12. `app/src/lib/palette.ts:34-35` — Overly complex rounding formula**
`Math.round(Math.round((s + Number.EPSILON) * 100 * 100) / 100)` is equivalent to `Math.round(s * 100)` for any value that chroma.js returns. The double-round with epsilon guard is defensive but obscures intent and the outer `Math.round` is redundant since the inner already rounds to integer percents.

**13. `app/src/components/About.tsx` — Inline `<div />` used as empty grid spacer (lines 700, 760)**
Two bare `<div />` elements are used as empty column fillers in a CSS grid layout. Per project CLAUDE.md guidelines: "Never use empty divs for layout spacing — use proper margin/padding instead." These could be replaced with `grid-column` positioning on the adjacent element.

**14. `app/src/components/controls/SliderBase.tsx:10` — Missing `React` import for `React.CSSProperties`**
`trackStyle?: React.CSSProperties` references `React` in the type annotation, but `React` is not imported. This works because Vite's JSX transform doesn't require the import for JSX, but the type reference to `React.CSSProperties` requires `React` to be in scope for TypeScript. This likely works currently only because TypeScript's module resolution finds it globally, but it should be an explicit `import type React from 'react'` or use `CSSProperties` from `react` directly.

**15. `app/src/components/About.tsx:87,88` — `Strip` component uses index as key**
`{colors.map((c, i) => <div key={i} .../>)}` and `{colors.map((_, i) => ... key={i})}` — using array index as key is fine here since the list is fixed-length and never reordered, but worth flagging for consistency with the rest of the codebase which uses stable identifiers.

---

### SUMMARY TABLE

| # | File | Line | Severity | Issue |
|---|------|------|----------|-------|
| 1 | lib/palette.ts | 9 | High | Division-by-zero guard is load-bearing but undocumented |
| 2 | About.tsx | 39–71 | High | Module-level constant ordering creates fragile forward dependency |
| 3 | hooks/useCopyToClipboard.ts | 9 | High | Unhandled clipboard API rejection |
| 4 | About.tsx | 315 | Medium | Hook returns JSX — anti-pattern |
| 5 | SaturationCurve.tsx + EffectiveSaturation.tsx | 11–15 | Medium | Duplicated chart constants and infrastructure |
| 6 | PaletteGrid.tsx + SaturationCurve.tsx + EffectiveSaturation.tsx | various | Medium | Toggle switch markup copy-pasted 6+ times |
| 7 | export/ExportDialog.tsx | 15 | Medium | formatPalette runs on every render even when dialog closed |
| 8 | About.tsx | 128–295 | Medium | ColorPickerPlane: 1600 SVG rects + Framer Motion cells with no memoization |
| 9 | About.tsx | 315–454 | Medium | useCurvePickerVisuals: 1600 cells without memoization |
| 10 | palette/PaletteGrid.tsx | 10,50 | Low | Magic number coupling between default count and palette generation |
| 11 | palette/PaletteStrip.tsx | 10 | Low | Unnecessary array spread+reverse on every render |
| 12 | lib/palette.ts | 34–35 | Low | Redundant double-round formula |
| 13 | About.tsx | 700,760 | Low | Empty divs used as CSS grid spacers (violates CLAUDE.md) |
| 14 | controls/SliderBase.tsx | 10 | Low | React.CSSProperties used without explicit React import |
| 15 | About.tsx | 87,88 | Low | Index used as key in Strip component maps |

</teammate-message>

<summary>Tool use: **SendMessage**</summary>

```text
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Message sent to team-lead's inbox",
  "routing": {
    "sender": "ux-reviewer",
    "senderColor": "green",
    "target": "@team-lead",

    "summary": "UX/accessibility review complete — structured findings",
    "content": "## UX & Accessibility Review — Chroma Neutrals\n\nReviewed all components in `app/src/components/` and `app/src/index.css`.\n\n---\n\n### HIGH severity\n\n**1. Toggle switches missing `aria-labelledby` / accessible name linkage**\n- Files: `PaletteGrid.tsx:98-110`, `PaletteGrid.tsx:113-127`, `SaturationCurve.tsx:92-104`, `EffectiveSaturation.tsx:79-91`\n- Each toggle `<button role=\"switch\">` is wrapped in a `<label>`, but the label's text is in a `<span>` sibling, not associated via `htmlFor`/`id` or `aria-labelledby`. Screen readers will announce the button with no meaningful name.\n- Fix: add `id` to each `<span>` label and `aria-labelledby` to the `<button>`, or wrap them in a proper `<label>` with `htmlFor` on a real `<input type=\"checkbox\">` converted to a switch.\n\n**2. Toggle switch touch/click targets are below minimum**\n- Files: `PaletteGrid.tsx:103`, `SaturationCurve.tsx:97`, `EffectiveSaturation.tsx:84`\n- The `<button>` is `w-5 h-2.5` (20×10px). Even the 24px web minimum is not met, let alone 44px for mobile.\n- Fix: increase hit area with padding or use a transparent pseudo-element overlay.\n\n**3. Hue gradient bar has no keyboard access**\n- File: `About.tsx:611-629` (`HuePicker` component)\n- The gradient `<div>` responds only to pointer events (`onPointerDown`, `onPointerMove`). It has no `role`, no `tabIndex`, no keyboard handler, and no `aria-*` attributes. Keyboard users cannot change the hue via this control.\n- Fix: add `role=\"slider\"`, `tabIndex={0}`, `aria-valuemin`, `aria-valuemax`, `aria-valuenow`, and `onKeyDown` handler for arrow keys.\n\n**4. Hue preset buttons in About have no visible focus style**\n- File: `About.tsx:571-582`\n- The preset color buttons lack `focus-visible:` ring or any focus indicator. Keyboard users get no visual feedback.\n- Fix: add `focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-white/50 focus-visible:ring-offset-1`.\n\n---\n\n### MEDIUM severity\n\n**5. `<label>` wrapping a `<select>` in PaletteGrid uses non-interactive wrapper text**\n- File: `PaletteGrid.tsx:71-84`, `PaletteGrid.tsx:85-95`\n- The `<label>` wraps both the description `<span>` and the `<select>`. This is actually correct HTML association, however the label text (\"swatches\", \"orientation\") has opacity `text-white/20` which is approximately 20% white on a dark background — contrast ratio is far below WCAG AA 4.5:1 for 9px text.\n\n**6. Subtitle and annotation text fails WCAG AA contrast**\n- Multiple locations:\n  - `Header.tsx:12` — `text-white/40` (\"Tinted neutral palettes…\") on `hsl(0 0% 6%)` ≈ 2.5:1\n  - `Header.tsx:19` — `text-white/30` (\"Click swatch to copy\") ≈ 1.9:1\n  - `PaletteGrid.tsx:72, 86, 97, 113` — `text-white/20` labels ≈ 1.4:1\n  - `SaturationCurve.tsx:91`, `EffectiveSaturation.tsx:78` — `text-white/20` ≈ 1.4:1\n  - `About.tsx:83, 116` — `text-[hsl(0_0%_50%)]` on `hsl(0 0% 6%)` background ≈ 3.5:1 (below AA for small text)\n- While decorative/supplementary text can be exempt, the \"Click swatch to copy\" instruction and control labels are functional and must meet contrast requirements.\n\n**7. `ColorSwatch` compact text sizes are illegibly small**\n- File: `ColorSwatch.tsx:26-28`\n- In compact mode (`compact=true`, triggered at `swatchCount > 24`), the label is `text-[9px]` and hex string is `text-[7px]`. These are below any legibility threshold and fail WCAG 1.4.4 (resize text) in practice.\n- Consider hiding labels in compact mode entirely rather than rendering at sub-8px sizes.\n\n**8. `AppShell` is a `<div>`, not `<main>` or landmark**\n- File: `AppShell.tsx:6`\n- The root wrapper is a `<div>`. There is no `<main>` landmark, so screen reader users cannot skip to main content. The page has no skip-navigation link.\n- Fix: change to `<main>` or add a `<main>` wrapping the content below the header.\n\n**9. `PaletteStrip` color swatches are decorative `<div>` elements with no accessible description**\n- File: `PaletteStrip.tsx:10-17`\n- The strip is purely visual and has no `role=\"presentation\"` / `aria-hidden=\"true\"` to suppress it from the accessibility tree, nor does it have an accessible label if it's meant to be meaningful.\n- Fix: add `aria-hidden=\"true\"` to the container `<div>` if decorative, or add a `role=\"img\"` with `aria-label` if it conveys information.\n\n**10. `ExportPreview` Copy/Download buttons have no `aria-label` describing what is being copied/downloaded**\n- File: `ExportPreview.tsx:17-30`\n- The buttons say \"Copy\" and \"Download\" but do not specify what. While acceptable in context, adding `aria-label=\"Copy palette code\"` and `aria-label=\"Download palette file\"` improves screen reader experience.\n\n**11. `CopyFeedback` animation not suppressed under `prefers-reduced-motion`**\n- File: `CopyFeedback.tsx:11-24`\n- The `index.css` correctly sets `transition-duration: 0.01ms` globally but the `motion.div` in `CopyFeedback` uses Framer Motion's `transition: { duration: 0.15 }`. Framer Motion's `LazyMotion` or `useReducedMotion` hook is not used, so the CSS rule does not suppress the JS-driven animation.\n- Fix: use Framer Motion's `useReducedMotion()` hook and conditionally set `transition={{ duration: 0 }}` when motion is reduced. This applies to all Framer Motion animations in `ExportDialog.tsx`, `About.tsx`, and `ColorPickerPlane` as well.\n\n---\n\n### LOW severity\n\n**12. `Dialog.Content` has no `aria-describedby`**\n- Files: `ExportDialog.tsx:39`, `About.tsx:678`\n- Both dialogs have a `Dialog.Title` but no `Dialog.Description` (or `aria-describedby`). Radix will warn and screen readers may not announce a description. Add `<Dialog.Description className=\"sr-only\">` with a brief purpose description.\n\n**13. FormatSelector `Tabs.List` is missing an `aria-label`**\n- File: `FormatSelector.tsx:49, 66`\n- Two tab groups exist without distinguishing `aria-label`s. Screen readers will announce both as generic \"tab list\". Add `aria-label=\"Export format\"` and `aria-label=\"Color space\"` to each `Tabs.List`.\n\n**14. `ControlBar` has no landmark role**\n- File: `ControlBar.tsx:7`\n- The sticky control bar is a `<div>`. Wrapping it in `<nav aria-label=\"Palette controls\">` or adding `role=\"region\" aria-label=\"Controls\"` would make it navigable via landmark shortcuts.\n\n**15. SVG charts use `role=\"img\"` with `aria-label` but no `<title>` element**\n- Files: `SaturationCurve.tsx:107-111`, `EffectiveSaturation.tsx:94-99`\n- Both SVGs have `role=\"img\"` and `aria-label`, which is correct. For maximum compatibility, also add a `<title>` element as the first child of `<svg>` (linked via `aria-labelledby`).\n\n**16. `About.tsx` hue reference swatch button (Popover trigger) has no accessible label**\n- File: `About.tsx:709-714`\n- The button that opens the hue picker popover has no `aria-label`. Screen readers will announce it as an unlabeled button.\n- Fix: add `aria-label=\"Change reference hue\"`.\n\n---\n\n### `index.css` Notes\n\n- `prefers-reduced-motion` media query is present and correct for CSS transitions/animations (lines 23–32).\n- No `prefers-color-scheme` support — the app is dark-only with no light mode fallback.\n- No `focus-visible` global baseline styles defined — relies entirely on each component's `focus-visible:` Tailwind classes, which are inconsistently applied (missing on several interactive elements as noted above).\n- No `:focus-visible` outline reset that might accidentally suppress default browser focus outlines — that's good.\n"
  }
}
```

<teammate-message teammate_id="ux-reviewer" color="green" summary="UX/accessibility review complete — structured findings">
## UX & Accessibility Review — Chroma Neutrals

Reviewed all components in `app/src/components/` and `app/src/index.css`.

---

### HIGH severity

**1. Toggle switches missing `aria-labelledby` / accessible name linkage**
- Files: `PaletteGrid.tsx:98-110`, `PaletteGrid.tsx:113-127`, `SaturationCurve.tsx:92-104`, `EffectiveSaturation.tsx:79-91`
- Each toggle `<button role="switch">` is wrapped in a `<label>`, but the label's text is in a `<span>` sibling, not associated via `htmlFor`/`id` or `aria-labelledby`. Screen readers will announce the button with no meaningful name.
- Fix: add `id` to each `<span>` label and `aria-labelledby` to the `<button>`, or wrap them in a proper `<label>` with `htmlFor` on a real `<input type="checkbox">` converted to a switch.

**2. Toggle switch touch/click targets are below minimum**
- Files: `PaletteGrid.tsx:103`, `SaturationCurve.tsx:97`, `EffectiveSaturation.tsx:84`
- The `<button>` is `w-5 h-2.5` (20×10px). Even the 24px web minimum is not met, let alone 44px for mobile.
- Fix: increase hit area with padding or use a transparent pseudo-element overlay.

**3. Hue gradient bar has no keyboard access**
- File: `About.tsx:611-629` (`HuePicker` component)
- The gradient `<div>` responds only to pointer events (`onPointerDown`, `onPointerMove`). It has no `role`, no `tabIndex`, no keyboard handler, and no `aria-*` attributes. Keyboard users cannot change the hue via this control.
- Fix: add `role="slider"`, `tabIndex={0}`, `aria-valuemin`, `aria-valuemax`, `aria-valuenow`, and `onKeyDown` handler for arrow keys.

**4. Hue preset buttons in About have no visible focus style**
- File: `About.tsx:571-582`
- The preset color buttons lack `focus-visible:` ring or any focus indicator. Keyboard users get no visual feedback.
- Fix: add `focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-white/50 focus-visible:ring-offset-1`.

---

### MEDIUM severity

**5. `<label>` wrapping a `<select>` in PaletteGrid uses non-interactive wrapper text**
- File: `PaletteGrid.tsx:71-84`, `PaletteGrid.tsx:85-95`
- The `<label>` wraps both the description `<span>` and the `<select>`. This is actually correct HTML association, however the label text ("swatches", "orientation") has opacity `text-white/20` which is approximately 20% white on a dark background — contrast ratio is far below WCAG AA 4.5:1 for 9px text.

**6. Subtitle and annotation text fails WCAG AA contrast**
- Multiple locations:
  - `Header.tsx:12` — `text-white/40` ("Tinted neutral palettes…") on `hsl(0 0% 6%)` ≈ 2.5:1
  - `Header.tsx:19` — `text-white/30` ("Click swatch to copy") ≈ 1.9:1
  - `PaletteGrid.tsx:72, 86, 97, 113` — `text-white/20` labels ≈ 1.4:1
  - `SaturationCurve.tsx:91`, `EffectiveSaturation.tsx:78` — `text-white/20` ≈ 1.4:1
  - `About.tsx:83, 116` — `text-[hsl(0_0%_50%)]` on `hsl(0 0% 6%)` background ≈ 3.5:1 (below AA for small text)
- While decorative/supplementary text can be exempt, the "Click swatch to copy" instruction and control labels are functional and must meet contrast requirements.

**7. `ColorSwatch` compact text sizes are illegibly small**
- File: `ColorSwatch.tsx:26-28`
- In compact mode (`compact=true`, triggered at `swatchCount > 24`), the label is `text-[9px]` and hex string is `text-[7px]`. These are below any legibility threshold and fail WCAG 1.4.4 (resize text) in practice.
- Consider hiding labels in compact mode entirely rather than rendering at sub-8px sizes.

**8. `AppShell` is a `<div>`, not `<main>` or landmark**
- File: `AppShell.tsx:6`
- The root wrapper is a `<div>`. There is no `<main>` landmark, so screen reader users cannot skip to main content. The page has no skip-navigation link.
- Fix: change to `<main>` or add a `<main>` wrapping the content below the header.

**9. `PaletteStrip` color swatches are decorative `<div>` elements with no accessible description**
- File: `PaletteStrip.tsx:10-17`
- The strip is purely visual and has no `role="presentation"` / `aria-hidden="true"` to suppress it from the accessibility tree, nor does it have an accessible label if it's meant to be meaningful.
- Fix: add `aria-hidden="true"` to the container `<div>` if decorative, or add a `role="img"` with `aria-label` if it conveys information.

**10. `ExportPreview` Copy/Download buttons have no `aria-label` describing what is being copied/downloaded**
- File: `ExportPreview.tsx:17-30`
- The buttons say "Copy" and "Download" but do not specify what. While acceptable in context, adding `aria-label="Copy palette code"` and `aria-label="Download palette file"` improves screen reader experience.

**11. `CopyFeedback` animation not suppressed under `prefers-reduced-motion`**
- File: `CopyFeedback.tsx:11-24`
- The `index.css` correctly sets `transition-duration: 0.01ms` globally but the `motion.div` in `CopyFeedback` uses Framer Motion's `transition: { duration: 0.15 }`. Framer Motion's `LazyMotion` or `useReducedMotion` hook is not used, so the CSS rule does not suppress the JS-driven animation.
- Fix: use Framer Motion's `useReducedMotion()` hook and conditionally set `transition={{ duration: 0 }}` when motion is reduced. This applies to all Framer Motion animations in `ExportDialog.tsx`, `About.tsx`, and `ColorPickerPlane` as well.

---

### LOW severity

**12. `Dialog.Content` has no `aria-describedby`**
- Files: `ExportDialog.tsx:39`, `About.tsx:678`
- Both dialogs have a `Dialog.Title` but no `Dialog.Description` (or `aria-describedby`). Radix will warn and screen readers may not announce a description. Add `<Dialog.Description className="sr-only">` with a brief purpose description.

**13. FormatSelector `Tabs.List` is missing an `aria-label`**
- File: `FormatSelector.tsx:49, 66`
- Two tab groups exist without distinguishing `aria-label`s. Screen readers will announce both as generic "tab list". Add `aria-label="Export format"` and `aria-label="Color space"` to each `Tabs.List`.

**14. `ControlBar` has no landmark role**
- File: `ControlBar.tsx:7`
- The sticky control bar is a `<div>`. Wrapping it in `<nav aria-label="Palette controls">` or adding `role="region" aria-label="Controls"` would make it navigable via landmark shortcuts.

**15. SVG charts use `role="img"` with `aria-label` but no `<title>` element**
- Files: `SaturationCurve.tsx:107-111`, `EffectiveSaturation.tsx:94-99`
- Both SVGs have `role="img"` and `aria-label`, which is correct. For maximum compatibility, also add a `<title>` element as the first child of `<svg>` (linked via `aria-labelledby`).

**16. `About.tsx` hue reference swatch button (Popover trigger) has no accessible label**
- File: `About.tsx:709-714`
- The button that opens the hue picker popover has no `aria-label`. Screen readers will announce it as an unlabeled button.
- Fix: add `aria-label="Change reference hue"`.

---

### `index.css` Notes

- `prefers-reduced-motion` media query is present and correct for CSS transitions/animations (lines 23–32).
- No `prefers-color-scheme` support — the app is dark-only with no light mode fallback.
- No `focus-visible` global baseline styles defined — relies entirely on each component's `focus-visible:` Tailwind classes, which are inconsistently applied (missing on several interactive elements as noted above).
- No `:focus-visible` outline reset that might accidentally suppress default browser focus outlines — that's good.

</teammate-message>

---

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-03-16 11:24:57Z)**_

Code quality review complete. I reviewed all 27 source files in `app/src/` and sent 15 findings to team-lead, ranging from high to low severity. Key issues found:

**High:** Unhandled clipboard rejection in `useCopyToClipboard`, load-bearing undocumented `sMod===0` guard, fragile module-level constant ordering in `About.tsx`

**Medium:** Hook returning JSX (`useCurvePickerVisuals`), heavily duplicated SVG chart infrastructure across `SaturationCurve` and `EffectiveSaturation`, 6+ copy-pasted toggle switch instances, `formatPalette` running unconditionally on all renders, 1600 SVG cells regenerated without memoization during drag interactions

**Low:** Empty div grid spacers violating CLAUDE.md, missing explicit React import for `CSSProperties`, implicit magic number coupling between palette count and UI defaults, redundant double-round formula

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-03-16 11:25:03Z)**_

Task #2 completed. Here's a summary of what was found across all components:

**16 issues identified** across 4 severity tiers:

**HIGH (4 issues)**
- Toggle switches (`role="switch"`) missing accessible name linkage — screen readers get no label
- Toggle switch hit targets are 20×10px — below the 24px web minimum
- Hue gradient bar in About is pointer-only, completely inaccessible to keyboard users
- Hue preset color buttons have no focus indicator

**MEDIUM (7 issues)**
- Control label text (`text-white/20`) has ~1.4:1 contrast ratio — far below AA
- Subtitle/annotation text throughout fails WCAG AA contrast (`text-white/30`, `/40`)
- Compact swatch text at 7–9px is illegibly small (WCAG 1.4.4)
- `AppShell` has no `<main>` landmark and no skip-nav link
- `PaletteStrip` decorative divs are exposed to accessibility tree without `aria-hidden`
- Export buttons lack descriptive `aria-label`s
- Framer Motion animations not suppressed by `prefers-reduced-motion` (CSS rule won't affect JS-driven animations)

**LOW (5 issues)**
- Both dialogs missing `Dialog.Description` / `aria-describedby`
- `FormatSelector` tab groups missing `aria-label` to distinguish them
- `ControlBar` has no landmark role
- SVG charts have `aria-label` but no `<title>` element for maximum compat
- About popover trigger button has no `aria-label`

```md
---
type: note
created: 2026-03-16
---

# Comprehensive Project Review

Full codebase review covering code quality, UX/accessibility, and export system correctness. Performed 2026-03-16 on branch `feat/export-palette`.

---

## Code Quality

### High

| # | File | Line | Issue |
|---|------|------|-------|
| CQ-1 | `lib/palette.ts` | 9 | Division-by-zero guard on `getSaturation` is load-bearing but undocumented — if removed, `sMod=0` causes `Infinity` |
| CQ-2 | `About.tsx` | 39–71 | Module-level constant ordering creates fragile forward dependency (`buildStrips` declared before `BOUNDARY_SCALE`) |
| CQ-3 | `hooks/useCopyToClipboard.ts` | 9 | Unhandled clipboard API promise rejection — no `.catch()`, no user error feedback |

### Medium

| # | File | Line | Issue |
|---|------|------|-------|
| CQ-4 | `About.tsx` | 315 | `useCurvePickerVisuals` hook returns JSX — anti-pattern, should return data to components |
| CQ-5 | `SaturationCurve.tsx` + `EffectiveSaturation.tsx` | 11–15 | Duplicated chart constants and SVG infrastructure (~80 lines each) |
| CQ-6 | `PaletteGrid.tsx` + curve components | various | Toggle switch markup copy-pasted 6+ times — extract shared component |
| CQ-7 | `export/ExportDialog.tsx` | 15 | `formatPalette` runs on every render even when dialog is closed |
| CQ-8 | `About.tsx` | 128–295 | `ColorPickerPlane`: 1600 SVG rects + Framer Motion cells with no memoization, re-renders on drag |
| CQ-9 | `About.tsx` | 315–454 | `useCurvePickerVisuals`: 1600 picker cells computed without memoization |

### Low

| # | File | Line | Issue |
|---|------|------|-------|
| CQ-10 | `palette/PaletteGrid.tsx` | 10, 50 | Magic number `98` couples default swatch count to palette generation implicitly |
| CQ-11 | `palette/PaletteStrip.tsx` | 10 | Unnecessary `[...entries].reverse()` on every render |
| CQ-12 | `lib/palette.ts` | 34–35 | Overly complex double-round formula — outer `Math.round` is redundant |
| CQ-13 | `About.tsx` | 700, 760 | Empty `<div />` used as CSS grid spacers (violates CLAUDE.md guideline) |
| CQ-14 | `controls/SliderBase.tsx` | 10 | `React.CSSProperties` used without explicit `React` import |
| CQ-15 | `About.tsx` | 87, 88 | Array index used as key in `Strip` component maps |

---

## UX & Accessibility

### High

| # | File | Line | Issue |
|---|------|------|-------|
| UX-1 | `PaletteGrid.tsx`, `SaturationCurve.tsx`, `EffectiveSaturation.tsx` | various | Toggle switches (`role="switch"`) have no accessible name — `<span>` label not linked via `aria-labelledby` |
| UX-2 | `PaletteGrid.tsx`, `SaturationCurve.tsx`, `EffectiveSaturation.tsx` | various | Toggle switch hit area is 20×10px — below both 24px web and 44px mobile minimums |
| UX-3 | `About.tsx` | 611–629 | Hue gradient bar has no keyboard access — no `role="slider"`, no `tabIndex`, no arrow key handler |
| UX-4 | `About.tsx` | 571–582 | Hue preset buttons have no visible focus style for keyboard navigation |

### Medium

| # | File | Line | Issue |
|---|------|------|-------|
| UX-5 | `PaletteGrid.tsx` | 71–95 | `<label>` wrapper text at `text-white/20` (~1.4:1 contrast) fails WCAG AA |
| UX-6 | Multiple (`Header.tsx`, `PaletteGrid.tsx`, `SaturationCurve.tsx`, `About.tsx`) | various | Functional text at `text-white/20` to `text-white/40` fails WCAG AA contrast (1.4:1 – 2.5:1 vs required 4.5:1) |
| UX-7 | `ColorSwatch.tsx` | 26–28 | Compact mode renders labels at 9px/7px — below legibility threshold |
| UX-8 | `AppShell.tsx` | 6 | No `<main>` landmark — screen readers cannot skip to content |
| UX-9 | `PaletteStrip.tsx` | 10–17 | Color strip has no `aria-hidden` (decorative) or `role="img"` with label (informative) |
| UX-10 | `ExportPreview.tsx` | 17–30 | Copy/Download buttons lack `aria-label` describing the action target |
| UX-11 | `CopyFeedback.tsx`, `ExportDialog.tsx`, `About.tsx` | various | Framer Motion animations not suppressed under `prefers-reduced-motion` — CSS rule doesn't affect JS-driven transitions |

### Low

| # | File | Line | Issue |
|---|------|------|-------|
| UX-12 | `ExportDialog.tsx`, `About.tsx` | 39, 678 | Dialogs missing `Dialog.Description` / `aria-describedby` |
| UX-13 | `FormatSelector.tsx` | 49, 66 | Two tab groups lack distinguishing `aria-label` |
| UX-14 | `ControlBar.tsx` | 7 | Sticky control bar has no landmark role |
| UX-15 | `SaturationCurve.tsx`, `EffectiveSaturation.tsx` | SVG elements | SVG charts have `role="img"` + `aria-label` but no `<title>` element |
| UX-16 | `About.tsx` | 709–714 | Hue popover trigger button has no `aria-label` |

---

## Export System

### Medium

| # | File | Line | Issue |
|---|------|------|-------|
| EX-1 | `lib/export.ts` | 17–18 | HCL `c2` (chroma channel) not guarded for NaN like hue is — inconsistent pattern |

### Low

| # | File | Line | Issue |
|---|------|------|-------|
| EX-2 | `lib/export.ts` | 116–117 | GPL format: `c.rgb()` returns floats — `String(r)` on a float breaks column alignment. Needs `Math.round()` |
| EX-3 | `lib/export.ts` | 77–82 | TW v3 output has over-indented inner lines (cosmetic, 10-space indent vs expected 6) |
| EX-4 | `lib/export.ts` | 74, 94, 103 | Hard-coded `"grey-"` prefix strip tightly couples to `generateNeutrals` label format |
| EX-5 | `lib/export.ts` | 148 | Generic `text/plain` MIME type for all downloads — more specific types would improve browser behavior |
| EX-6 | `hooks/useCopyToClipboard.ts` | 9 | No `.catch()` on clipboard write — silent failure on permission denied (same as CQ-3) |
| EX-7 | `export/FormatSelector.tsx` | 45–73 | `Tabs.Root` without `Tabs.Content` — ARIA `aria-controls` points to non-existent panels. Consider `role="radiogroup"` |

---

## Summary

| Category | High | Medium | Low | Total |
|----------|------|--------|-----|-------|
| Code Quality | 3 | 6 | 6 | 15 |
| UX / A11y | 4 | 7 | 5 | 16 |
| Export System | 0 | 1 | 6 | 7 |
| **Total** | **7** | **14** | **17** | **38** |

### Recommended Priority Actions

1. **Fix clipboard error handling** (CQ-3 / EX-6) — affects both palette copy and export copy
2. **Add accessible names to toggle switches** (UX-1) — screen readers get no label
3. **Increase toggle switch hit area** (UX-2) — currently 20×10px
4. **Add keyboard access to hue gradient bar** (UX-3) — completely inaccessible to keyboard users
5. **Fix WCAG contrast on functional labels** (UX-5, UX-6) — raise from `white/20` to at least `white/60`
6. **Memoize heavy SVG generation in About.tsx** (CQ-8, CQ-9) — 3200+ elements recomputed on drag
7. **Extract shared toggle switch component** (CQ-6) — 6+ duplicated instances
8. **Fix GPL float RGB values** (EX-2) — concrete data correctness bug

```

```md
type: epic
created: 2026-03-16
status: open

# Accessibility & ARIA Compliance

Address all high and medium accessibility findings from NOTE-0001. The app currently has several WCAG violations: missing accessible names on interactive controls, no keyboard access on custom widgets, missing landmarks, and Framer Motion animations that ignore `prefers-reduced-motion`.

## Scope

- Extract shared `ToggleSwitch` component with proper ARIA and hit areas (UX-1, UX-2, CQ-6)
- Add keyboard access to hue gradient bar in About modal (UX-3)
- Add visible focus styles to hue preset buttons (UX-4)
- Add semantic landmarks (`<main>`, `<nav>`, `aria-hidden`) across shell components (UX-8, UX-9, UX-14)
- Add `aria-label` / `aria-describedby` to export buttons, dialogs, tab groups, popover trigger (UX-10, UX-12, UX-13, UX-15, UX-16)
- Suppress Framer Motion animations under `prefers-reduced-motion` (UX-11)

## Tasks

- TASK-0001
- TASK-0002
- TASK-0003
- TASK-0004
- TASK-0005
- TASK-0006
```

```md
# WCAG Contrast & Legibility

Multiple functional UI labels and controls fail WCAG AA contrast requirements. Control labels use `text-white/20` (~1.4:1) where 4.5:1 is required for small text. Compact swatch labels render at 7–9px which is below any legibility threshold.

## Scope

- Raise contrast on all functional labels across Header, PaletteGrid, SaturationCurve, EffectiveSaturation, About (UX-5, UX-6)
- Fix or hide compact swatch labels that render below 8px (UX-7)

## Tasks

- TASK-0007
- TASK-0008
```

```md
type: epic

# Performance Optimization

The About modal generates 3200+ SVG elements (two 40×40 grids) without memoization, recomputing on every hue drag event. The export dialog runs `formatPalette` on every store update even when closed. Several minor unnecessary re-computations exist.

## Scope

- Memoize `ColorPickerPlane` SVG cell generation (CQ-8)
- Memoize `useCurvePickerVisuals` picker cell generation (CQ-9)
- Defer `formatPalette` computation until export dialog is open (CQ-7)
- Memoize `PaletteStrip` reverse operation (CQ-11)

## Tasks

- TASK-0009
- TASK-0010
- TASK-0011
```

```md
# Extract shared ToggleSwitch component with proper ARIA and hit area

The custom toggle switch (`<button role="switch">`) is copy-pasted 6+ times across `PaletteGrid.tsx`, `SaturationCurve.tsx`, and `EffectiveSaturation.tsx`. None have accessible names and the hit area (20×10px) is below the 24px minimum.

## Requirements

- Create `components/controls/ToggleSwitch.tsx` accepting `label`, `checked`, `onChange` props
- Link label text via `aria-labelledby` or use a proper `<label>` with `htmlFor`
- Increase hit area to at least 24×24px (44px preferred for mobile) using padding or transparent overlay
- Replace all 6+ inline toggle instances with the shared component
- Ensure keyboard operation (Space/Enter to toggle)

## Files to modify

- `app/src/components/controls/ToggleSwitch.tsx` (new)
- `app/src/components/palette/PaletteGrid.tsx`
- `app/src/components/curves/SaturationCurve.tsx`
- `app/src/components/curves/EffectiveSaturation.tsx`

```

```md
# Add keyboard access to hue gradient bar

The hue gradient bar in `About.tsx` (`HuePicker` component, lines 611–629) responds only to pointer events. Keyboard users cannot interact with it.

## Requirements

- Add `role="slider"` to the gradient `<div>`
- Add `tabIndex={0}` for focusability
- Add `aria-valuemin={0}`, `aria-valuemax={360}`, `aria-valuenow={hue}`, `aria-label="Reference hue"`
- Add `onKeyDown` handler: Left/Down arrow decreases hue, Right/Up increases hue (step of 1, shift+arrow for step of 10)
- Add visible focus style (`focus-visible:ring-2`)

## Files to modify

- `app/src/components/About.tsx` (HuePicker component)
```

```md
# Add semantic landmarks to shell components

Screen readers cannot navigate by landmarks. The app has no `<main>`, the control bar has no `<nav>`, and the palette strip is neither marked decorative nor labeled.

## Requirements

- `AppShell.tsx`: Change root `<div>` to `<main>` or wrap content in `<main>`
- `ControlBar.tsx`: Wrap in `<nav aria-label="Palette controls">` or add `role="region"` with label
- `PaletteStrip.tsx`: Add `aria-hidden="true"` to container (decorative overview strip)

## Files to modify

- `app/src/components/AppShell.tsx`
- `app/src/components/controls/ControlBar.tsx`
- `app/src/components/palette/PaletteStrip.tsx`
```

```md
---
type: task
```

```md
status: open
epic: EPIC-0001
priority: medium
refs: [UX-11]
---

# Suppress Framer Motion animations under prefers-reduced-motion

The CSS `prefers-reduced-motion` rule in `index.css` correctly handles CSS transitions but does not affect Framer Motion's JS-driven animations. Dialogs, copy feedback, and About modal animations still play for users who prefer reduced motion.

## Requirements

- Use Framer Motion's `useReducedMotion()` hook or `MotionConfig` provider
- Option A (preferred): Wrap app in `<MotionConfig reducedMotion="user">` in `main.tsx` — Framer Motion will automatically respect the OS preference
- Option B: Use `useReducedMotion()` in each animated component and conditionally set `transition={{ duration: 0 }}`
- Verify: `ExportDialog.tsx`, `About.tsx` (dialog + ColorPickerPlane animations), `CopyFeedback.tsx`

## Files to modify

- `app/src/main.tsx` (if using MotionConfig approach)
- Or individual component files

```


```md
---
type: task

epic: EPIC-0002
priority: medium
refs: [UX-5, UX-6]
---

# Fix WCAG AA contrast on functional labels

Functional UI labels across multiple components use opacity values (`text-white/20` to `text-white/40`) that fail WCAG AA 4.5:1 contrast ratio on the dark background (`hsl(0 0% 6%)`).

## Affected locations

- `Header.tsx:12` — `text-white/40` subtitle (~2.5:1)
- `Header.tsx:19` — `text-white/30` copy instruction (~1.9:1)
- `PaletteGrid.tsx:72, 86, 97, 113` — `text-white/20` control labels (~1.4:1)
- `SaturationCurve.tsx:91` — `text-white/20` label (~1.4:1)
- `EffectiveSaturation.tsx:78` — `text-white/20` label (~1.4:1)
- `About.tsx:83, 116` — `text-[hsl(0_0%_50%)]` (~3.5:1)

## Requirements

- Raise all functional labels to at least `text-white/60` (~5.2:1) or find the minimum opacity that achieves 4.5:1 on the dark background
- Decorative/supplementary text can remain lower contrast per WCAG exceptions, but control labels and instructional text must meet AA
- Maintain the subdued aesthetic — use the minimum contrast that meets AA rather than making everything bright

```

```md
# Fix compact swatch label legibility

When `swatchCount > 24`, `ColorSwatch` renders in compact mode with labels at `text-[9px]` and hex values at `text-[7px]`. These are below any practical legibility threshold.

## Requirements

- Option A (preferred): Hide labels entirely in compact mode (`showLabels={false}`) and rely on hover tooltips
- Option B: Set a minimum font size floor of 10px for any visible text
- Ensure the swatch remains clickable to copy even without visible labels
- Consider showing label on hover via a tooltip when labels are hidden

## Files to modify

- `app/src/components/palette/ColorSwatch.tsx`
- `app/src/components/palette/PaletteGrid.tsx` (adjust `showLabels` logic)

```

```md
# Memoize heavy SVG generation in About.tsx

`ColorPickerPlane` builds 1600 `<rect>` elements plus 40 Framer Motion boundary cells on every render. `useCurvePickerVisuals` builds another 1600 picker cells. Both recompute on every hue change during drag (continuous pointer events).

## Requirements

- Wrap `ColorPickerPlane`'s cell arrays (`cells`, `boundaryCells`, `curveDots`, `curvePoints`) in `useMemo` keyed on `hue`
- Wrap `useCurvePickerVisuals`'s `pickerCells`, `curveData`, and path computations in `useMemo` keyed on `hue`
- Consider `React.memo` on `ColorPickerPlane` component itself
- Profile before/after to verify improvement (optional but recommended)

## Files to modify

- `app/src/components/About.tsx`

```

```md
# Defer formatPalette until export dialog is open

`ExportDialog` calls `formatPalette(entries, format, space)` on every render, even when `open === false`. Since the dialog is always mounted in the Header, this runs on every store update.

## Requirements

- Wrap in `useMemo` keyed on `[entries, format, space]`
- Or conditionally compute only when `open === true` (return empty string when closed)
- The second approach is slightly better since it avoids even memoized computation when the dialog is never opened

## Files to modify

- `app/src/components/export/ExportDialog.tsx`

```

```md
# Memoize PaletteStrip reverse operation

`PaletteStrip` creates `[...entries].reverse()` on every render. Since `entries` comes from `usePalette()` which is already memoized, the reverse can also be memoized.

## Requirements

- Wrap in `useMemo(() => [...entries].reverse(), [entries])`

## Files to modify

- `app/src/components/palette/PaletteStrip.tsx`
```

```md
priority: high
refs: [CQ-1, CQ-2]
---

# Document getSaturation guard and fix About.tsx constant ordering

Two related issues around code clarity and safety:

1. `getSaturation` in `lib/palette.ts:9` has a division-by-zero guard (`if (sMod === 0) return 1`) that is load-bearing — removing it causes `Infinity`. The guard needs a comment explaining why it exists and what `sMod=0` means semantically.

2. `About.tsx` declares `buildStrips` (line 39) before `BOUNDARY_SCALE` (line 71) which it references. This works at runtime since `buildStrips` is only called during render, but the ordering is fragile and confusing.

## Requirements

- Add a comment above the `sMod === 0` guard explaining: "sMod=0 means no curve adjustment — return baseline multiplier. Also prevents division by zero."
- Reorder `About.tsx` module-level constants so dependencies flow top-to-bottom: `LIGHTNESS_LABELS` → `BOUNDARY_SCALE` → `buildStrips`

## Files to modify

- `app/src/lib/palette.ts`
- `app/src/components/About.tsx`

```

```text

```

```md
# Refactor useCurvePickerVisuals to return data, not JSX

The `useCurvePickerVisuals` hook in `About.tsx` returns JSX elements (`curveGraph`, `rotatedPicker`). Hooks should return data; components should render. This makes the hook untestable and prevents memoization of returned JSX.

## Requirements

- Extract the two returned JSX blocks into proper components (e.g., `CurveGraph` and `RotatedPicker`)
- Have the hook return computed data (paths, cells, coordinates) that the components consume as props
- Or eliminate the hook entirely and move the computation into the components themselves

## Files to modify

- `app/src/components/About.tsx`
```

```md
# Extract shared chart infrastructure from curve components

`SaturationCurve.tsx` and `EffectiveSaturation.tsx` share identical constants (`W`, `H`, `PAD`, `PLOT_W`, `PLOT_H`), identical `toSvgX`/`toSvgY` coordinate transforms, and identical SVG grid/axis rendering markup (~80 lines each).

## Requirements

- Create shared constants in a `curves/chartConstants.ts` or similar
- Create a `useChartCoords(width, height, padding)` hook returning `toSvgX`, `toSvgY`
- Consider a `<ChartContainer>` component that renders the common SVG grid, axes, and labels
- Replace duplicated code in both curve components with the shared abstractions

- `app/src/components/curves/chartConstants.ts` (new)
- `app/src/components/curves/SaturationCurve.tsx`
- `app/src/components/curves/EffectiveSaturation.tsx`
```

```md
# Add clipboard error handling with user feedback

`useCopyToClipboard` calls `navigator.clipboard.writeText().then(...)` with no `.catch()`. If the clipboard API fails (permissions denied, non-HTTPS, browser restriction), the failure is silently swallowed with no user feedback.

## Requirements

- Add `.catch()` to the clipboard write promise
- On failure, provide user feedback (e.g., set a different state like `"error"` that the UI can show as "Failed to copy")
- Return an error state from the hook so consumers can react
- Consider fallback to `document.execCommand('copy')` for older browsers (optional, low priority)

## Files to modify

- `app/src/hooks/useCopyToClipboard.ts`
- Potentially `app/src/components/palette/CopyFeedback.tsx` (if adding error state UI)
- Potentially `app/src/components/export/ExportPreview.tsx` (if showing error state)

```


```md
# Fix GPL float RGB and color space NaN guards

Two correctness issues in `lib/export.ts`:

1. **EX-2** GPL format uses `c.rgb()` which returns floats. `String(127.0000001).padStart(3)` produces misaligned columns. Needs `Math.round()` on each channel.
2. **EX-1** HCL color value guards hue for NaN (`h || 0`) but not chroma (`c2`). While chroma is typically 0 for achromatic (not NaN), the guard pattern should be consistent. Apply `|| 0` to all channels that could be NaN.

## Requirements

- `formatGPL`: Change to `Math.round(r)`, `Math.round(g)`, `Math.round(b)` before `String().padStart(3)`
- `colorValue` HCL case: Add `|| 0` guard to `c2` for consistency
- `colorValue` OKLCH case: Verify `c2` (chroma) is also guarded

## Files to modify

- `app/src/lib/export.ts`

```

```text
File created successfully at: /Users/grant/code/chroma-colours-neutrals/board/tasks/TASK-0017_fix-gpl-and-nan-guards.md
```

```md
# Replace Tabs with radiogroup in FormatSelector

`FormatSelector` uses `@radix-ui/react-tabs` purely for the tab-bar UI with no `Tabs.Content` panels. This means `aria-controls` points to non-existent panel elements, producing incomplete ARIA semantics.

## Requirements

- Replace `Tabs.Root`/`Tabs.List`/`Tabs.Trigger` with a custom button group using `role="radiogroup"` and `role="radio"` on each option
- Or use `@radix-ui/react-toggle-group` which better fits the single-selection pattern
- Maintain the same visual appearance (pill buttons in a rounded container)
- Ensure `aria-checked` state is set correctly on the active option

## Files to modify

- `app/src/components/export/FormatSelector.tsx`

```

<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead" summary="EPIC-0005: Export system polish">
You are working on EPIC-0005 (Export System Polish) for the chroma-colours-neutrals project. You're in an isolated worktree.

First, create and checkout a new branch: `git checkout -b fix/epic-0005-export-polish`

Then implement these 4 tasks. After each task, commit with conventional commit format. Run `cd app && npm run build && npm run lint` after all changes to verify.

## TASK-0016: Clipboard error handling
File: `app/src/hooks/useCopyToClipboard.ts`
- Add `.catch()` to `navigator.clipboard.writeText(text).then(...)`
- On failure, set copiedLabel to null (don't show success) and log to console.warn
- The hook should also return an `error` state: `const [error, setError] = useState<string | null>(null)`
- Clear error on next successful copy

## TASK-0017: Fix GPL float RGB and NaN guards
File: `app/src/lib/export.ts`
- In `formatGPL`: wrap each RGB channel with `Math.round()` before `String().padStart(3)`
- In `colorValue` HCL case (around line 17-18): add `|| 0` guard to `c2` (chroma) for consistency with hue guard
- In `colorValue` OKLCH case: verify `c2` is also guarded with `|| 0`

## TASK-0018: Export output polish
File: `app/src/lib/export.ts`
- TW v3 formatter: Fix indentation. The `colors:` value should have its inner JSON indented at 6 spaces consistently. Simplify the approach — build the object string manually or adjust the indent/prefix math.
- Replace `e.label.replace("grey-", "")` with `String(e.lightness)` in formatTailwindV3, formatJS, and formatJSON — uses the numeric lightness value directly instead of string manipulation.
- Update `FORMAT_EXTENSIONS` approach: create a `FORMAT_MIME_TYPES` map and use specific MIME types: `text/css` for css/tw4/scss, `text/javascript` for tw3/js, `application/json` for json, `text/plain` for text/gpl. Use these in the Blob constructor in `downloadPalette`.

## TASK-0019: Replace Tabs with toggle group in FormatSelector
File: `app/src/components/export/FormatSelector.tsx`
- Replace `@radix-ui/react-tabs` (Tabs.Root/List/Trigger) with a custom button group using `role="radiogroup"` on the container and `role="radio"` + `aria-checked` on each button
- Maintain the same visual appearance (pill buttons in rounded container)
- Apply to both the format selector and color space selector
- Add `aria-label="Export format"` and `aria-label="Color space"` to each radiogroup container

After all tasks, run build and lint to verify. Make one commit per task with format `fix(export): description`.
</teammate-message>

<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./app/src/hooks/useCopyToClipboard.ts`

```text
     1→import { useState, useCallback, useRef } from "react";
     2→
     3→export function useCopyToClipboard(timeout = 1500) {
     4→  const [copiedLabel, setCopiedLabel] = useState<string | null>(null);
     5→  const timerRef = useRef<ReturnType<typeof setTimeout>>(undefined);
     6→
     7→  const copy = useCallback(
     8→    (text: string, label: string) => {
     9→      navigator.clipboard.writeText(text).then(() => {
    10→        setCopiedLabel(label);
    11→        clearTimeout(timerRef.current);
    12→        timerRef.current = setTimeout(() => setCopiedLabel(null), timeout);
    13→      });
    14→    },
    15→    [timeout],
    16→  );
    17→
    18→  return { copiedLabel, copy };
    19→}
    20→
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./app/src/lib/export.ts`

```text
     1→import chroma from "chroma-js";
     2→import type { ColorEntry, ExportFormat, ColorSpace } from "../types/palette";
     3→
     4→function colorValue(entry: ColorEntry, space: ColorSpace): string {
     5→  const c = chroma(entry.hex);
     6→  switch (space) {
     7→    case "hsl":
     8→      return `hsl(${entry.hslString})`;
     9→    case "hex":
    10→      return entry.hex;
    11→    case "rgb": {
    12→      const [r, g, b] = c.rgb();
    13→      return `rgb(${r}, ${g}, ${b})`;
    14→    }
    15→    case "hcl": {
    16→      const [h, c2, l] = c.hcl();
    17→      const hR = Math.round(h || 0);
    18→      const cR = Math.round(c2 * 10) / 10;
    19→      const lR = Math.round(l * 10) / 10;
    20→      return `hcl(${hR}, ${cR}, ${lR})`;
    21→    }
    22→    case "oklch": {
    23→      const [l, c2, h] = c.oklch();
    24→      const lR = Math.round(l * 1000) / 1000;
    25→      const cR = Math.round(c2 * 1000) / 1000;
    26→      const hR = Math.round(h || 0);
    27→      return `oklch(${lR} ${cR} ${hR})`;
    28→    }
    29→  }
    30→}
    31→
    32→export function formatPalette(
    33→  entries: ColorEntry[],
    34→  format: ExportFormat,
    35→  space: ColorSpace,
    36→): string {
    37→  switch (format) {
    38→    case "css":
    39→      return formatCSS(entries, space);
    40→    case "tw4":
    41→      return formatTailwindV4(entries, space);
    42→    case "tw3":
    43→      return formatTailwindV3(entries, space);
    44→    case "scss":
    45→      return formatSCSS(entries, space);
    46→    case "js":
    47→      return formatJS(entries, space);
    48→    case "json":
    49→      return formatJSON(entries, space);
    50→    case "gpl":
    51→      return formatGPL(entries);
    52→    case "text":
    53→      return formatText(entries, space);
    54→  }
    55→}
    56→
    57→function formatCSS(entries: ColorEntry[], space: ColorSpace): string {
    58→  const vars = entries
    59→    .map((e) => `  --${e.label}: ${colorValue(e, space)};`)
    60→    .join("\n");
    61→  return `:root {\n${vars}\n}`;
    62→}
    63→
    64→function formatTailwindV4(entries: ColorEntry[], space: ColorSpace): string {
    65→  const vars = entries
    66→    .map((e) => `  --color-${e.label}: ${colorValue(e, space)};`)
    67→    .join("\n");
    68→  return `@theme {\n${vars}\n}`;
    69→}
    70→
    71→function formatTailwindV3(entries: ColorEntry[], space: ColorSpace): string {
    72→  const colors: Record<string, string> = {};
    73→  for (const e of entries) {
    74→    const num = e.label.replace("grey-", "");
    75→    colors[num] = colorValue(e, space);
    76→  }
    77→  const inner = JSON.stringify({ grey: colors }, null, 4)
    78→    .split("\n")
    79→    .map((line) => `      ${line}`)
    80→    .join("\n")
    81→    .trim();
    82→  return `/** @type {import('tailwindcss').Config} */\nmodule.exports = {\n  theme: {\n    extend: {\n      colors: ${inner},\n    },\n  },\n};`;
    83→}
    84→
    85→function formatSCSS(entries: ColorEntry[], space: ColorSpace): string {
    86→  return entries
    87→    .map((e) => `$${e.label}: ${colorValue(e, space)};`)
    88→    .join("\n");
    89→}
    90→
    91→function formatJS(entries: ColorEntry[], space: ColorSpace): string {
    92→  const colors: Record<string, string> = {};
    93→  for (const e of entries) {
    94→    const num = e.label.replace("grey-", "");
    95→    colors[num] = colorValue(e, space);
    96→  }
    97→  return `export const grey = ${JSON.stringify(colors, null, 2)} as const;`;
    98→}
    99→
   100→function formatJSON(entries: ColorEntry[], space: ColorSpace): string {
   101→  const tokens: Record<string, { $value: string; $type: string }> = {};
   102→  for (const e of entries) {
   103→    const num = e.label.replace("grey-", "");
   104→    tokens[num] = {
   105→      $value: colorValue(e, space),
   106→      $type: "color",
   107→    };
   108→  }
   109→  return JSON.stringify({ grey: tokens }, null, 2);
   110→}
   111→
   112→function formatGPL(entries: ColorEntry[]): string {
   113→  const lines = ["GIMP Palette", "Name: Grey Neutrals", `Columns: ${entries.length}`, "#"];
   114→  for (const e of entries) {
   115→    const c = chroma(e.hex);
   116→    const [r, g, b] = c.rgb();
   117→    lines.push(`${String(r).padStart(3)} ${String(g).padStart(3)} ${String(b).padStart(3)}\t${e.label}`);
   118→  }
   119→  return lines.join("\n");
   120→}
   121→
   122→function formatText(entries: ColorEntry[], space: ColorSpace): string {
   123→  const maxLabel = Math.max(...entries.map((e) => e.label.length));
   124→  return entries
   125→    .map((e) => `${e.label.padEnd(maxLabel)}  ${colorValue(e, space)}`)
   126→    .join("\n");
   127→}
   128→
   129→const FORMAT_EXTENSIONS: Record<ExportFormat, string> = {
   130→  css: "css",
   131→  tw4: "css",
   132→  tw3: "js",
   133→  scss: "scss",
   134→  js: "ts",
   135→  json: "json",
   136→  gpl: "gpl",
   137→  text: "txt",
   138→};
   139→
   140→export function downloadPalette(
   141→  entries: ColorEntry[],
   142→  format: ExportFormat,
   143→  space: ColorSpace,
   144→): void {
   145→  const content = formatPalette(entries, format, space);
   146→  const ext = FORMAT_EXTENSIONS[format];
   147→  const blob = new Blob([content], { type: "text/plain;charset=utf-8" });
   148→  const url = URL.createObjectURL(blob);
   149→  const a = document.createElement("a");
   150→  a.href = url;
   151→  a.download = `palette.${ext}`;
   152→  a.click();
   153→  URL.revokeObjectURL(url);
   154→}
   155→
```
</details></tool-use>
